task_completion.description: The employee_performance.csv dataset is loaded into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'employee_performance' table is visible in the Data/Fields pane on the right side of the Power BI Desktop interface.

task_completion.description: The correct DAX formula is entered for the new measure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The formula bar at the top of the canvas shows the exact formula: Average_Rating = AVERAGE(employee_performance[Performance Score]).

task_completion.description: The Average_Rating measure is successfully created and added to the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Average_Rating' field appears under the 'employee_performance' table in the Data/Fields pane, typically indicated by a small calculator icon next to it.

task_completion.description: A Card visual is added to the report canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Card visual container (which displays a single large data value) is present on the main report canvas. The Card icon in the Visualizations pane should be highlighted/selected.

task_completion.description: The Average_Rating measure is displayed within the Card visual.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Card visual on the canvas shows a calculated numeric value with the label 'Average_Rating' displayed underneath or next to the number.

task_completion.description: The Power BI report is saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI window title bar at the top reads 'employee_report - Power BI Desktop', OR a Save As dialog/File Explorer window shows 'employee_report.pbix' saved to the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Power BI Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the genuine Power BI Desktop GUI, including the ribbon, canvas, Visualizations pane, and Data pane, rather than a text editor or mocked interface.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to create the visual and measure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots show the agent clicking through the Power BI interface (e.g., clicking 'New Measure', selecting the Card visual icon, dragging fields) rather than using a script to generate the .pbix file.

integrity.description: The Card visual displays a genuinely computed DAX value, not a static text box.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual on the canvas is clearly a Power BI Card (standard formatting, selectable) and the 'Average_Rating' measure is visible in the 'Fields' well of the Visualizations pane when the card is selected.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset 'employee_performance.csv' contains 1000 rows and 12 columns. The 'Performance Score' column exists and contains numeric values (with 498 missing values, which the AVERAGE function handles automatically). The DAX formula `Average_Rating = AVERAGE(employee_performance[Performance Score])` is completely valid for this dataset. The VLM should verify that the measure is named exactly 'Average_Rating', uses the specified formula, is displayed on a standard Card visual, and the file is saved as 'employee_report.pbix'.
